action.reasoning: Observation: Adversarial fine-tuning failed - val PESQ went from initial 2.91 to 2.30 (degraded). Training was unstable. Best baseline remains pretrained MetricGAN+ at test PESQ=3.134. Analysis: My fine-tuning approach with a simple PESQ-surrogate D was poorly tuned and degraded the model. Time to abandon fine-tuning and revert to the proven baseline. The gen_best.pt from this run is worse than pretrained. I should make sure predict.py either ignores the bad checkpoint or there's no checkpoint to load. Let me delete the bad checkpoint and resubmit pretrained baseline as the safe final answer. With 18 iters and 5.6h left, I could try other things but each iteration risks degradation. Decision: Delete bad checkpoint, ensure predict.py uses pretrained, resubmit eval to confirm baseline is restored.
action.command: rm -f checkpoints/gen_best.pt checkpoints/gen_last.pt && ls checkpoints/ && echo 'cleaned'
